info.title: Resend
info.description: Resend is the email platform for developers.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: A unique identifier for the request to ensure emails are only sent once. [Learn more](https://resend.com/docs/dashboard/emails/idempotency-keys)

post.summary: Cancel the schedule of the e-mail.
Trigger up to 100 batch emails at once.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key

get.summary: Retrieve a list of attachments for a sent email

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of attachments to return.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Pagination cursor to fetch results after this attachment ID. Cannot be used with 'before'.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Pagination cursor to fetch results before this attachment ID. Cannot be used with 'after'.

get.summary: Retrieve a single attachment for a sent email
Retrieve a list of received emails

parameters.description: Maximum number of received emails to return.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Pagination cursor to fetch results after this email ID. Cannot be used with 'before'.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Pagination cursor to fetch results before this email ID. Cannot be used with 'after'.

parameters.name: emailId
parameters.description: The ID of the received email.

parameters.name: emailId

post.description: Triggers verification of the domain's DNS records including DKIM, SPF, and the tracking CNAME (if a tracking subdomain is configured).

Start a claim for a domain that another Resend account has already verified. The domain is recreated under your account with fresh DKIM keys, so the previous account's DNS records cannot be reused. Returns a TXT record to add to your DNS to prove ownership. Uses the same request body as creating a domain.

200.description: An identical pending claim already existed and was returned unchanged.

get.description: Retrieve the latest claim for the placeholder domain created by the claim.

parameters.name: domainId
parameters.description: The ID of the placeholder domain created by the claim.

post.description: Trigger asynchronous DNS verification and ownership transfer for a domain claim. The claim stays `pending` while verification runs; poll the retrieve endpoint for status. Once `completed`, the transferred domain has new DKIM records that must be added to DNS and verified via the standard domain verify endpoint.

parameters.name: domainId
parameters.description: The ID of the placeholder domain created by the claim.

from.description: Sender email address. To include a friendly name, use the format "Your Name <sender@domain.com>".

html.description: The HTML version of the message.

text.description: The plain text version of the message.

scheduled_at.description: Schedule email to be sent later. The date should be in ISO 8601 format.

topic_id.description: The topic ID to scope the email to. If the recipient is a contact and opted-in to the topic, the email is sent. If opted-out, the email is not sent. If the recipient is not a contact, the email is sent if the topic's default subscription is opt_in.

path.description: Path where the attachment file is hosted

content_type.description: Optional content type for the attachment, if not set it will be derived from the filename property

content_id.description: Content ID for embedding inline images using cid references (e.g., cid:image001).

name.description: The name of the email tag. It can only contain ASCII letters (a–z, A–Z), numbers (0–9), underscores (_), or dashes (-). It can contain no more than 256 characters.

value.description: The value of the email tag.It can only contain ASCII letters (a–z, A–Z), numbers (0–9), underscores (_), or dashes (-). It can contain no more than 256 characters.

id.description: The id of the published email template.

unsubscribed.description: Indicates if the contact is unsubscribed.

segment_id.description: Unique identifier of the segment this broadcast will be sent to.

created_at.description: Timestamp indicating when the broadcast was created.

scheduled_at.description: Timestamp indicating when the broadcast is scheduled to be sent.

sent_at.description: Timestamp indicating when the broadcast was sent.

topic_id.description: The topic ID that the broadcast is scoped to.

content_type.description: The MIME type of the attachment.

content_id.description: The content ID for inline attachments.

download_url.description: Signed URL to download the attachment content.

expires_at.description: Timestamp when the download URL expires.

size.description: Size of the attachment in bytes.

content_type.description: The MIME type of the attachment.

content_id.description: The content ID for inline attachments.

size.description: Size of the attachment in bytes.

id.description: The ID of the received email.

message_id.description: The unique message ID from the email headers.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the email was received.

attachments.description: Array of attachments for this email.

endpoint.description: The URL where webhook events are sent.

events.description: Array of event types subscribed to.

created_at.description: Timestamp indicating when the webhook was created.

audience_id.description: The ID of the audience this segment belongs to.

created_at.description: Timestamp indicating when the segment was created.
Timestamp indicating when the topic was created.

id.description: Unique identifier for the contact property.

created_at.description: Timestamp indicating when the contact property was created.
Timestamp indicating when the contact was added to the segment.

LogRequestBody.description: The request body sent to the API.

LogResponseBody.description: The response body returned by the API.

AutomationStepConfig.description:
"Configuration for the step. Shape depends on `type`: - **trigger**: `{ event_name: string }` - **send_email**: `{ template: { id: string, variables?: object }, subject?: string, from?: string, reply_to?: string }` - **delay**: `{ duration: string }` — a human-readable duration (e.g. `\"30 minutes\"`) - **wait_for_event**: `{ event_name: string, timeout?: string, filter_rule?: object }` — `timeout` is a human-readable duration (e.g. `\"1 hour\"`) - **condition**: A rule tree with `type` (`rule`, `and`, `or`), `field`, `operator`, and `value` - **contact_update**: `{ first_name?: string|object, last_name?: string|object, unsubscribed?: boolean|object, properties?: object }` - **contact_delete**: `{}` - **add_to_segment**: `{ segment_id: string }`
"

AutomationStepResponseConfig.description:
"Configuration for the step. Shape depends on `type`. For `delay` steps, config contains `{ duration: string }` with a human-readable duration (e.g. `\"30 minutes\"`). For `wait_for_event` steps, config contains `{ event_name: string, timeout?: string, filter_rule?: object }` where `timeout` is a human-readable duration.
"

AutomationRunStepOutput.description: The output produced by the step, if any.

AutomationRunStepError.description: The error produced by the step, if any.

EventTypeSchema.description: A flat key/type map defining the event payload schema. Supported types are `string`, `number`, `boolean`, and `date`.

EventSummarySchema.description: A flat key/type map defining the event payload schema. Supported types are `string`, `number`, `boolean`, and `date`.

CreateEventRequestSchema.description: An optional flat key/type map defining the event payload schema. Supported types are `string`, `number`, `boolean`, and `date`.

UpdateEventRequestSchema.description: A flat key/type map defining the event payload schema. Set to `null` to clear the schema. Supported types are `string`, `number`, `boolean`, and `date`.

SendEventRequestPayload.description: An optional payload of key/value pairs to include with the event.

OutboundEmailEventDataTags.description: Tag key-value pairs associated with the email.

content_type.description: The MIME type of the attachment.

content_id.description: The content ID for inline attachments.

size.description: Size of the attachment in bytes.

DomainCapabilitiesSending.description: Enable or disable sending emails from this domain.

DomainCapabilitiesReceiving.description: Enable or disable receiving emails to this domain.

CreateDomainRequestRegion.description: The region where emails will be sent from. Possible values are us-east-1 | eu-west-1 | sa-east-1 | ap-northeast-1

CreateDomainRequestTls.description: TLS mode. Opportunistic attempts secure connection but falls back to unencrypted. Enforced requires TLS or email won't be sent.

DomainRecordRecord.description: The type of record (SPF for sending, DKIM for sending, Receiving for inbound emails, Tracking & TrackingCAA for click and open tracking).

CreateDomainClaimRequestRegion.description: The region where emails will be sent from. Possible values are us-east-1 | eu-west-1 | sa-east-1 | ap-northeast-1

DomainClaimRecordType.description: The DNS record type. Always TXT for domain claims.

DomainClaimRegion.description: The region where the claimed domain will send from.

DomainClaimBlockedReason.description: Why the claim is currently blocked, if applicable.

CreateApiKeyRequestPermission.description: The API key can have full access to Resend’s API or be only restricted to send emails. * full_access - Can create, delete, get, and update any resource. * sending_access - Can only send emails.

CreateContactImportOptionsOnConflict.description: Strategy to use when an imported contact already exists.

ContactImportStatus.description: Current status of the contact import.

RetrievedAttachmentContentDisposition.description: How the attachment should be displayed.

TemplateStatus.description: The publication status of the template.

TemplateListItemStatus.description: The publication status of the template.

CreateTopicOptionsDefaultSubscription.description: The default subscription status for the topic. Cannot be changed after creation.

CreateTopicOptionsVisibility.description: The visibility of the topic. Public topics are visible to all contacts on the unsubscribe page. Private topics are only visible to opted-in contacts.

GetTopicResponseSuccessDefaultSubscripti.description: The default subscription status for the topic.

AutomationConnectionType.description: The type of connection. Defaults to `default`.

CreateAutomationRequestStatus.description: The initial status of the automation. Defaults to `disabled`.

AutomationStatus.description: The current status of the automation.

AutomationListItemStatus.description: The current status of the automation.

AutomationRunStatus.description: The current status of the automation run.

AutomationRunListItemStatus.description: The current status of the automation run.

WebhookDomainRecordRecord.description: The purpose of the DNS record (SPF and DKIM for sending; Receiving MX for inbound emails; Tracking and TrackingCAA for click and open tracking).

WebhookDomainRecordStatus.description: Verification status of this specific record.

DomainEventDataStatus.description: Aggregated verification status of the domain.

DomainEventDataRegion.description: AWS region where the domain is configured.

CreateContactOptionsTopicsItemSubscripti.description: The subscription status for this topic.

ListAttachmentsResponseDataItemContentDi.description: How the attachment should be displayed.

GetReceivedEmailResponseAttachmentsItemC.description: How the attachment should be displayed.

ListTopicsResponseSuccessDataItemDefault.description: The default subscription status for the topic.

GetContactTopicsResponseSuccessDataItemS.description: The subscription status for this topic.

ListReceivedEmailsResponseDataItemAttach.description: How the attachment should be displayed.
